name: Hydrogen
description: Hydrogen is the lightest and most abundant element in the universe. It is a colorless, odorless gas that forms water when burned with oxygen.

name: Helium
description: Helium is a light, colorless, and inert noble gas. It has the lowest boiling point of any element and is often used in balloons and airships.

name: Lithium
description: Lithium is a soft, silvery-white alkali metal. It is the lightest solid element and is crucial for rechargeable batteries and mood-stabilizing drugs.

name: Beryllium
description: Beryllium is a hard, grayish metal with a high melting point. It is lightweight and non-magnetic, used in aerospace components and X-ray windows.

name: Boron
description: Boron is a dark, brittle metalloid essential for plant cell walls. It is used in borosilicate glass (Pyrex) and as a semiconductor dopant.

name: Carbon
description: Carbon is the chemical basis of all known life. It forms diverse allotropes including diamond, graphite, and fullerenes.

name: Nitrogen
description: Nitrogen is a colorless, odorless gas making up 78% of Earth's atmosphere. It is essential for DNA, proteins, and fertilizers.

name: Oxygen
description: Oxygen is a highly reactive gas essential for respiration and combustion. It is the most abundant element in Earth's crust.

name: Fluorine
description: Fluorine is the most electronegative and reactive of all elements. It is a pale yellow gas that forms compounds with almost all other elements.

name: Neon
description: Neon is a colorless, odorless, inert noble gas that glows reddish-orange in a vacuum discharge tube. It is famously used in advertising signs.

name: Sodium
description: Sodium is a soft, silvery-white, highly reactive metal. It is essential for nerve function and reacts vigorously with water to form sodium hydroxide and hydrogen gas.

name: Magnesium
description: Magnesium is a shiny gray solid with a high strength-to-weight ratio. It is essential for chlorophyll in plants and burns with a brilliant white flame.

name: Aluminium
description: Aluminium is a lightweight, silvery-white metal with excellent corrosion resistance. It is the most abundant metal in Earth's crust and widely used in packaging and construction.

name: Silicon
description: Silicon is a hard, brittle crystalline solid and the second most abundant element in Earth's crust. It is the fundamental semiconductor material used in computer chips and electronics.

name: Phosphorus
description: Phosphorus exists in several allotropic forms including white, red, and black. It is essential for DNA, ATP, and bones, and is a key component of fertilizers.

name: Sulfur
description: Sulfur is a bright yellow, brittle solid with a distinctive odor when burned. It is essential for amino acids and is widely used in vulcanizing rubber and producing sulfuric acid.

name: Chlorine
description: Chlorine is a yellow-green gas with a pungent, irritating odor. It is a powerful disinfectant used in water treatment and forms common table salt (NaCl) with sodium.

name: Argon
description: Argon is a colorless, odorless, inert noble gas. It is the third most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere and is used as a protective shield gas in welding and light bulbs.

name: Potassium
description: Potassium is a soft, silvery metal that tarnishes rapidly in air. It is essential for nerve transmission and muscle function in living organisms.

name: Calcium
description: Calcium is a soft gray metal and the fifth most abundant element in Earth's crust. It is essential for bones, teeth, and shells, and is a key component of limestone and cement.

name: Scandium
description: Scandium is a silvery-white metal that develops a yellowish tint upon exposure to air. It is used in aerospace alloys and high-intensity stadium lighting.

name: Titanium
description: Titanium is a strong, lustrous metal with low density and high corrosion resistance. It is used in aircraft, spacecraft, and medical implants.

name: Vanadium
description: Vanadium is a hard, silvery-gray, ductile metal. It is primarily used as a steel additive to increase strength and corrosion resistance in tools and engines.

name: Chromium
description: Chromium is a hard, brittle, lustrous metal with a high polish. It is essential for stainless steel production and gives rubies and emeralds their characteristic colors.

name: Manganese
description: Manganese is a hard, brittle, gray-white metal essential for iron and steel production. It is also a vital trace nutrient for all known living organisms.

name: Iron
description: Iron is the most common element on Earth by mass and the fourth most abundant in the crust. It is essential for hemoglobin in blood and is the primary component of steel.

name: Cobalt
description: Cobalt is a hard, lustrous, blue-gray metal used in high-strength alloys and lithium-ion batteries. It is also the central atom in vitamin B12.

name: Nickel
description: Nickel is a silvery-white metal with a slight golden tinge. It is highly resistant to corrosion and is widely used in stainless steel and coinage.

name: Copper
description: Copper is a soft, malleable, and ductile metal with very high thermal and electrical conductivity. It is essential for electrical wiring and plumbing.

name: Zinc
description: Zinc is a slightly brittle metal at room temperature with a shiny-gray appearance. It is primarily used for galvanizing iron and steel to prevent rusting.

name: Gallium
description: Gallium is a soft, silvery metal that melts just above room temperature. It is used in semiconductors and LEDs, particularly gallium arsenide for solar panels and lasers.

name: Germanium
description: Germanium is a lustrous, hard, grayish-white metalloid. It was used in early transistors and is now important in fiber optics, infrared optics, and polymerization catalysts.

name: Arsenic
description: Arsenic is a brittle, steel-gray metalloid notorious for its toxicity. Despite this, it is used as a semiconductor dopant and in wood preservatives.

name: Selenium
description: Selenium exists in several allotropic forms and is an essential micronutrient for animals. It has photoconductive properties, making it useful in photocopiers and solar cells.

name: Bromine
description: Bromine is a fuming, red-brown liquid at room temperature and the only nonmetal that is liquid. It is used in flame retardants and water purification compounds.

name: Krypton
description: Krypton is a colorless, odorless, inert noble gas. It is used in high-performance fluorescent lamps and photographic flashes.

name: Rubidium
description: Rubidium is a soft, silvery-white metallic element that ignites spontaneously in air. It is used in atomic clocks, GPS systems, and medical imaging.

name: Strontium
description: Strontium is a soft, silvery metal that burns with a brilliant red flame. It is used in fireworks and flares, and the radioactive isotope strontium-90 is a fallout product.

name: Yttrium
description: Yttrium is a silvery-metallic transition metal. It is used in phosphors for LED displays and as a component in yttrium aluminum garnet (YAG) lasers.

name: Zirconium
description: Zirconium is a lustrous, grayish-white, corrosion-resistant metal. It is used in nuclear reactors as cladding for fuel rods due to its low neutron absorption.

name: Niobium
description: Niobium is a soft, gray, ductile transition metal often found with tantalum. It is used in superconducting magnets and high-strength steel alloys.

name: Molybdenum
description: Molybdenum is a silvery metal with a very high melting point. It is essential as a trace element for nitrogen fixation in plants and is used in high-strength steel alloys.

name: Technetium
description: Technetium was the first element to be produced artificially. All its isotopes are radioactive, and it is used in medical diagnostic imaging for bone scans.

name: Ruthenium
description: Ruthenium is a hard, white metal and one of the platinum group metals. It is used as a catalyst and as a hardener in platinum and palladium alloys.

name: Rhodium
description: Rhodium is a rare, silvery-white, and highly reflective metal. It is primarily used in catalytic converters to reduce toxic emissions from vehicle exhaust.

name: Palladium
description: Palladium is a rare, silvery-white metal belonging to the platinum group. It is widely used in catalytic converters, electronics, and hydrogen purification.

name: Silver
description: Silver is a soft, white, lustrous metal with the highest electrical and thermal conductivity of any element. It has been valued for coinage, jewelry, and photography for millennia.

name: Cadmium
description: Cadmium is a soft, bluish-white toxic metal. It is used in rechargeable nickel-cadmium batteries and as a protective plating on steel.

name: Indium
description: Indium is a soft, silvery metal that produces a high-pitched scream when bent. It is essential for touchscreen displays and solar panels as indium tin oxide (ITO).

name: Tin
description: Tin is a soft, pliable, silvery-white metal resistant to corrosion. It is primarily used as a coating for steel cans and as a component of solder and bronze alloys.

name: Antimony
description: Antimony is a lustrous, gray metalloid known since ancient times. It is used as a flame retardant in plastics and textiles and as an alloying agent in lead batteries.

name: Tellurium
description: Tellurium is a brittle, silvery-white metalloid with a metallic luster. It is used in solar panels as cadmium telluride and in rewritable optical discs.

name: Iodine
description: Iodine is a dark gray, lustrous solid that sublimes into a violet gas. It is essential for thyroid hormones in vertebrates and is widely used as a disinfectant.

name: Xenon
description: Xenon is a dense, colorless, odorless noble gas. It is used in high-intensity arc lamps, ion thrusters for spacecraft, and as a general anesthetic.

name: Caesium
description: Caesium is a soft, golden-yellow alkali metal and one of the most reactive elements. It is used in atomic clocks, which define the international standard for the second.

name: Barium
description: Barium is a soft, silvery-white alkaline earth metal. Barium sulfate is opaque to X-rays and is used in medical 'barium meals' for gastrointestinal imaging.

name: Lanthanum
description: Lanthanum is a soft, ductile, silvery-white metal that tarnishes rapidly in air. It is the prototype for the lanthanide series and is used in camera lenses and carbon arc lamps.

name: Cerium
description: Cerium is the most abundant of the rare earth metals. It is used in catalytic converters, glass polishing, and the flints in cigarette lighters.

name: Praseodymium
description: Praseodymium is a soft, silvery metal that develops a green oxide coating in air. It is used in high-strength alloys for aircraft engines and to color glass and ceramics yellow.

name: Neodymium
description: Neodymium is a hard, silvery metal that tarnishes in air. It is essential for the strongest permanent magnets (NdFeB), used in headphones, hard drives, and electric motors.

name: Promethium
description: Promethium is the only radioactive lanthanide. It is extremely rare in nature and is used in atomic batteries for spacecraft and pacemakers.

name: Samarium
description: Samarium is a moderately hard, silvery metal. It is used in samarium-cobalt magnets, which retain magnetism at high temperatures, and in cancer treatment.

name: Europium
description: Europium is the most reactive rare earth metal and oxidizes instantly in air. It is used as a red phosphor in LED televisions and euro banknotes' anti-counterfeiting marks.

name: Gadolinium
description: Gadolinium is a silvery-white, ductile metal with unusual metallurgic properties. It is used as a contrast agent in MRI scans and in nuclear reactor control rods.

name: Terbium
description: Terbium is a soft, silvery rare earth metal. It is used in green phosphors for fluorescent lamps and LED displays, and in solid-state devices like the 'Terfenol-D' magnetostrictive alloy.

name: Dysprosium
description: Dysprosium is a soft, bright silver metal. It is added to neodymium magnets to improve their high-temperature performance, crucial for electric vehicle motors and wind turbines.

name: Holmium
description: Holmium has the highest magnetic permeability of any element. It is used in powerful magnets and holmium lasers for non-invasive medical procedures like kidney stone removal.

name: Erbium
description: Erbium is a soft, silvery metal. Erbium-doped fiber amplifiers are crucial for long-distance fiber optic communication, boosting light signals without converting them to electricity.

name: Thulium
description: Thulium is the least abundant of the stable lanthanides. It is used in portable X-ray devices that require no external power source and in high-efficiency lasers.

name: Ytterbium
description: Ytterbium is a soft, silvery metal. It is used as a dopant in stainless steel to improve grain refinement and in highly accurate atomic clocks.

name: Lutetium
description: Lutetium is the hardest and densest of the lanthanides. It is used as a catalyst in petroleum refining and in lutetium-based PET scanner detectors for medical imaging.

name: Hafnium
description: Hafnium is a lustrous, silvery-gray metal chemically similar to zirconium. It is used in nuclear reactor control rods due to its high neutron absorption.

name: Tantalum
description: Tantalum is a hard, blue-gray, corrosion-resistant metal. It is widely used in electronic capacitors and surgical implants.

name: Tungsten
description: Tungsten has the highest melting point of all metals and is extremely dense. It is used in light bulb filaments and cutting tools.

name: Rhenium
description: Rhenium is a dense, silvery-white metal with one of the highest melting points. It is used in high-temperature superalloys for jet engines.

name: Osmium
description: Osmium is the densest naturally occurring element and has a bluish-white lustre. It is used in fountain pen tips and electrical contacts.

name: Iridium
description: Iridium is a hard, brittle, and highly corrosion-resistant platinum-group metal. It is associated with the asteroid impact that caused dinosaur extinction.

name: Platinum
description: Platinum is a dense, malleable, precious gray-white metal highly resistant to corrosion. It is used in catalytic converters, jewelry, and laboratory equipment.

name: Gold
description: Gold is a dense, soft, yellow precious metal that does not tarnish. It has been used for coinage, jewelry, and electronics throughout history.

name: Mercury
description: Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature. It is highly toxic and was historically used in thermometers and barometers.

name: Thallium
description: Thallium is a soft, gray metal that is highly toxic. It is used in electronics, specialized glass, and as a rat poison.

name: Lead
description: Lead is a heavy, dense, soft metal with a bluish-white tint when freshly cut. It is used in batteries, radiation shielding, and ammunition.

name: Bismuth
description: Bismuth is a brittle, crystalline metal with a pinkish tinge and iridescent oxide layer. It is used in cosmetics, medicines, and low-melting alloys.

name: Polonium
description: Polonium is a rare, highly radioactive element discovered by Marie Curie. It has been used as a heat source for space probes and in antistatic devices.

name: Astatine
description: Astatine is the rarest naturally occurring element on Earth. It is highly radioactive and has potential uses in targeted alpha-particle cancer therapy.

name: Radon
description: Radon is a colorless, odorless, radioactive noble gas produced by the decay of uranium. It is a significant health hazard when accumulated in buildings.

name: Francium
description: Francium is the most unstable naturally occurring element and is extremely rare. It has no commercial applications due to its intense radioactivity.

name: Radium
description: Radium is a luminous, highly radioactive metal discovered by Marie Curie. It was historically used in self-luminous paints for watches and instruments.

name: Actinium
description: Actinium is a soft, silvery-white radioactive metal. It glows faintly blue in the dark due to its intense radioactivity and is used as a neutron source.

name: Thorium
description: Thorium is a silvery, radioactive metal that is more abundant than uranium. It is a potential fuel for safer, cleaner nuclear reactors.

name: Protactinium
description: Protactinium is a dense, silvery-gray metal that readily reacts with oxygen and water vapor. It is one of the rarest and most expensive naturally occurring elements.

name: Uranium
description: Uranium is a dense, silvery-white metal and the heaviest naturally occurring element. It is the primary fuel for nuclear power plants and atomic weapons.

name: Neptunium
description: Neptunium is a silvery, radioactive metal and the first transuranic element. It is a byproduct of plutonium production and is used in neutron detection equipment.

name: Plutonium
description: Plutonium is a silvery metal that tarnishes in air and is highly radioactive. It is a key fissile material in nuclear weapons and spacecraft power sources.

name: Americium
description: Americium is a synthetic, silvery-white radioactive metal. It is commonly used in household smoke detectors as a source of alpha particles.

name: Curium
description: Curium is a hard, dense, silvery radioactive metal that glows purple in the dark. It was discovered at the University of California, Berkeley.

name: Berkelium
description: Berkelium is a soft, silvery-white radioactive metal produced in minute quantities. It has no practical applications outside of scientific research.

name: Californium
description: Californium is a strong neutron emitter. It is used to start up nuclear reactors and in neutron radiography to detect cracks in aircraft parts.

name: Einsteinium
description: Einsteinium is a soft, silvery, synthetic radioactive metal first discovered in the debris of the first hydrogen bomb test.

name: Fermium
description: Fermium is a highly radioactive synthetic element. It is produced in nuclear reactors and has no uses beyond fundamental research.

name: Mendelevium
description: Mendelevium is a synthetic, radioactive metal that can only be produced in particle accelerators. It was named after Dmitri Mendeleev, creator of the periodic table.

name: Nobelium
description: Nobelium is a synthetic, radioactive metal named after Alfred Nobel. It has no applications outside of scientific research.

name: Lawrencium
description: Lawrencium is the last element in the actinide series. It is synthetic, highly radioactive, and can only be produced in tiny quantities.

name: Rutherfordium
description: Rutherfordium is a synthetic, highly radioactive metal named after physicist Ernest Rutherford. It has no practical applications outside of scientific research.

name: Dubnium
description: Dubnium is a synthetic, radioactive element named after the Russian town of Dubna. It is produced only in minute amounts for nuclear physics studies.

name: Seaborgium
description: Seaborgium is a synthetic, radioactive element named after Nobel laureate Glenn T. Seaborg. It has been produced only in particle accelerators.

name: Bohrium
description: Bohrium is a synthetic, radioactive element named after physicist Niels Bohr. It is extremely unstable and exists only for fractions of a second.

name: Hassium
description: Hassium is a synthetic, highly radioactive element named after the German state of Hesse. It has no known uses beyond fundamental research.

name: Meitnerium
description: Meitnerium is a synthetic, radioactive element named after physicist Lise Meitner. It is produced only in trace quantities and decays rapidly.

name: Darmstadtium
description: Darmstadtium is a synthetic, extremely radioactive element named after the city of Darmstadt, Germany. It has no applications outside of research.

name: Roentgenium
description: Roentgenium is a synthetic, radioactive element named after Wilhelm Röntgen, discoverer of X-rays. Its most stable isotope lasts only seconds.

name: Copernicium
description: Copernicium is a synthetic, highly radioactive element named after astronomer Nicolaus Copernicus. It is produced in minute amounts and decays quickly.

name: Nihonium
description: Nihonium is a synthetic, extremely radioactive element named after Japan (Nihon). It was first synthesized by a Japanese team at RIKEN.

name: Flerovium
description: Flerovium is a superheavy, synthetic, radioactive element named after the Flerov Laboratory in Russia. It is extremely unstable and difficult to study.

name: Moscovium
description: Moscovium is a synthetic, radioactive element named after the Moscow region. It exists for only a fraction of a second before decaying.

name: Livermorium
description: Livermorium is a synthetic, superheavy radioactive element named after Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory. It has no uses beyond research.

name: Tennessine
description: Tennessine is a synthetic, highly radioactive halogen named after the state of Tennessee. It is the second-heaviest known element.

name: Oganesson
description: Oganesson is a synthetic, superheavy noble gas and the heaviest known element. It is named after physicist Yuri Oganessian and exists for only milliseconds.
